column1: # 夢：冴子に飲み込まれるシーンの隠喩（口の中）

column2: In the sea on a stormy night, I float alone on the surface of the dark waters.
Instead of stars, endless rows of cumulus clouds cover the sky.
The pale light of the full moon peeks out from between two rows. Its brilliance makes me blink...
Then, several moons fill my vision. They form an arc, like beads on a string.
The water acts as a mirror, reflecting the round lights to encompass the world above and below with dozens of moons.
Suddenly, a huge wave appears. The current carries my body toward one of the moons.
Just as the harsh white light pouring down is about to pierce through my body--
The ocean floor opens up and I swirl down the whirlpool. I reach up with all my might and touch something soft...
I leap up with a start and find Chio plopped down on the flat floor in front of me.
How was it sleeping with me?
The corners of Chio's lips turn up as she smooths out the creases in her long skirt.
Look at the mess you've made... Did sleeping with me feel that good?
I hurriedly inspect myself. There are traces of saliva around my mouth.
Um... I just had my head on your lap and fell asleep. That's all that happened, right?
Teehee, let's go with that. It'd make you feel better, wouldn't it?
It's just a joke. The only things that touched were your head and my lap. You can relax.
It looked like you were having trouble sleeping, so I took matters into my own hands. So? Did you sleep a little better?
Er, I guess I slept fine, but I had a strange dream...
I find myself at a loss for words.
Oh, right! I became the supervisor, Saeko came back, a-and then, Moko and Taki...
I can see two images vividly in my mind--the sudden deaths of Moko and Taki, who had grown familiar to me.
Moko had been tossed into Saeko's mouth like a snack. Taki had been crushed between Saeko's fingers, his remains promptly removed by a tissue.
I'd watched their lives end so swiftly from the drawer beneath them. To be honest, I still can't quite believe it.
But no matter how I search for them in the dim drawer, they are nowhere to be found...
Are the two of them really dead...?
You saw what happened to Taki, and Moko must be digested by now. There aren't any doctors here to perform an autopsy, let alone any bodies to be autopsied.
Chio's tone is incredibly matter of fact.
I mean... That's just how it is.
...*sigh* I suppose this is a good chance to explain things properly.
I'm sure you already know this, but Saeko raises little people for her own purposes.
Protection? That's a bald-faced lie. She uses some kind of magic to shrink people that catch her eye around town and brings them back here.
Sometimes she just watches or plays with them, like dolls in a dollhouse, but her main goal is to eat them.
She stores the little people she catches in this drawer. Just like how you might clean sand from clams or keep crabs in a box.
Why are both of your examples seafood?
Do you remember what Taki said yesterday? About how the supervisor's job is to give items to the little people in the drawer.
It isn't completely true, but it isn't completely false either.
The supervisor's main goal is to raise appealing little people that Saeko will want to eat right up.
If you give a little person food, they'll become more appealing. Maybe they'll be more energetic, or they'll have a bit of extra flavor.
When their Appeal reaches 50, Saeko will eat them.
Fattening up a little person until they're yummy enough to give to Saeko--that's your real job as the supervisor.
Yesterday, when Taki kept trying to make me give Moko the peanut...
Yup. That was to increase the glasses girl's Appeal and have Saeko eat her up.
Th-Then, if I hadn't given Moko the peanut, she wouldn't have been killed?
No. I already told you--it's the supervisor's job to raise appealing little people.
If you don't do your job and nobody makes yummy little people, Saeko will get mad. And she'll kill all of us.
She threatened Taki with that before...and I don't think she was lying.
You chose the only method that could save the other little people without making Saeko angry.
Their deaths aren't your fault. They're nobody's fault. You just did the best you could in the situation you were put in.
B-But that's how I got Moko...
You have to stop thinking like that. Nobody's to blame for anyone's death in this drawer. But...
If you can't stop yourself from overthinking...
Just relax those shoulders and let yourself loose.
I'll teach you how to stop thinking about anything at all...

column1: # 画面暗転する。ゲーム中には書かれないけど、実際にはチオとリンがセックスしている

column2:
Oh, right. There are some little newcomers.
Why don't you go say hi?
...Wait. Were other people near us this whole time?

Yup... Wait, you didn't notice? Here I thought
you just liked having an audience.
I push Chio's hand away and put distance between us. I can feel my cheeks growing hot...
I look around the drawer. There really are other little people here. I don't know how I feel about talking to new people right now...but I should say hi.
A quiet-looking boy stands in the corner of the drawer. He's fidgeting, like he can't relax.
The boy looks up in surprise.
But the moment his eyes meet mine, his cheeks flush, and he turns his head away.
Um... Did you see that whole thing earlier? Sorry if you did.
I-It's fine. I'm just, um, not used to this kind of thing, but maybe it's n-normal around here...
I-I wouldn't want to get in the way of anybody else's fun...
Ah... Thanks for the consideration, I guess...
But, uh, could I ask your name?
M-My name is Maru. You're...Rin, right?
Yes...but how do you know that?
A-Anyway, could I help you with anything? I'm actually kind of in charge of this drawer.
Anything I need help with? Hm...
Oh, then, um, could I ask you for some advice? Only if you have the time...
I get chilly quite easily. When I stand around without moving, my hands and feet just get so cold.
I think I would be fine if I could just wear another layer. Is there anything I could use around here?
You mean some clothing? Maybe not. I've actually been having the same problem.
Oh... Then, what does everyone do when they need to change clothes?
Well, I actually just got here yesterday...
I decide not to think about Chio.
I see... Things must be quite tough.
How about fabric? Maybe cloth or paper. Anything is fine, as long as it's thin and flexible.
I like to make clothes, so I could try using fabric to make something to wear.
The new boy, Maru, seems to like making clothes. I'll look for something that he can use as fabric to put together a warm outfit.
I found some tissue... Would this work as a fabric?
Let's see... Paper fibers keep warmth in well, and tissue doesn't crease easily. This is perfect.
Could you use this as a fabric?
Hmm... Sorry, I can't imagine using this to make clothing.
From Maru's reaction, this won't be any good for clothing. I should look for something thinner and more flexible.
Could you use this as a fabric?
Rin? Excuse me, but who are you talking to?
I know I don't have much of a presence, but...
I gave it to the wrong person! I need to be more careful about who I talk to next, so that I don't hurt Maru further.
I think I can make a good outfit with this. I'll start right now.
With this shape, I can put these edges together, yes...
Maru moves both his hands skillfully. The tissue starts to build up into a complex shape, as if he is folding an origami crane.
Before I know it, the tissue has become a comfortable cape. It even has cute frills and a bow.
Wow! You're nimble with your fingers.
You look cute in that, Maru.
I feel a bit warmer now, thanks to you. Would you like me to make you something too, Rin? Maybe a scarf?
With that petite face and lovely skin, I'm sure you look wonderful in anything you wear.

column1: # TODO: 名前を「？？？」として表示する

column2: (I can feel a hungry gaze from the wall...)
Uh... I'm fine, thank you. I appreciate the offer.
So, um, what do you think? Have you gotten used to this place?
Yes, I think so. Though being shrunk so suddenly was a shock...
Saeko has been so kind. She told me she'd find a way to change me back, so I'm not too worried.
A-And I was anxious at first since there are so many people here, but I think things will be fine.
(Well, as long as he's relaxed now, I guess...)
I feel strangely anxious as I leave Maru to his clothes making. I hear a sigh from near the wall that seems to be equal parts relief and dismay.
A woman leans on the wall of the drawer with one foot up against it. For some reason, she's glaring in my direction.
Um, I guess I don't really want anything...
Hmph. Could you not talk to me then?
B-But we're going to be living here together. I think it'd be better...if I introduced myself...
You think you're the boss of some part-timers or something? Acting all goody-goody and doing pointless stuff only you care about.
When you're just a precocious li'l fuckboi who'll hump anything anywhere.
Fuckboi. Don't make me say it again.
I can't believe you did that in public. I'd rather die. That giant woman's nuts too. This place sucks.
What? You wanna say something? You thought I'd feel bad and say sorry if you looked all mopey?
No, that's not what I... Uh...
Could I ask for your name? I'm Rin.
I'm Kina. Cool name, right? Cooler than yours, anyway.
Um, so... Do you want anything? If there's anything you need, I'll get it for you.
...And now you're trying to bribe me? You're the worst.
Well, if you're offering, get me something that'll help me escape from here.
What else would anyone stuck in here need? Is screwing all you have on your mind?
My voice trails off. Kina's sharp gaze is focused on my face.
But she doesn't need to trap us in here to protect us, right?
I hate quiet places like this. Plus, the music here stinks. I want to get outta here, and fast.
There must be a hole or a gap or something. Else we'd all choke and die.
Can't see anything in the floor... Which means it's probably up there, but it's hard to see from here.
I want to check with my own eyes. Could you get me something big to climb up on?
(Saeko will probably get mad if I tell her I gave a little person something to help them escape...)
(But if Kina is just using it to look up... I'll just do what she says for now.)
Kina thinks there must be some kind of gap in the ceiling. I don't want her mood to get any worse, so I'll find her something big to climb on.
What do you think about this...?
The hell am I supposed to do with this?
There must be something bigger than you and me in here. Go take another look around.
Who do you think you're talking to?
That scared of talking to me? In that case... Ahaha, just give up already.
I gave the item to the wrong person! I need to give Kina something she can climb.
How about this iPod? You could climb up using the cable.
You any good at climbing trees? Just asking.
Um... Probably not. I don't think I've even tried before.
Figures. I'll go then. Get in a few push-ups while I'm gone.
With that, Kina tucks the earphone cable between her legs...
...and climbs up the cable like it's a vine.
Kina deftly reaches the top of the iPod in no time.
Of course I am! But... Ah...
I'm looking at the ceiling now...
Kina slides down the side of the iPod in disappointment. Then, she gets up like nothing happened at all.
No luck. There wasn't any gap up there.
I'll have to think of something else. I'll let you know when I do, so go fuck all you want.
Um, Rin... Are you free right now?
I turn around in surprise at the voice from behind me. As expected, Chio stands there with a smile.
What's with that serious look? Don't tell me you've found somebody else to be your partner?
...Oh, Rin. There's something I haven't told you about your job as the supervisor.
I don't want to tell you here, so could you come over to the wall? ...And we can continue where we left off, if you like.
Kina waves goodbye with a smirk, and I head back to the center of the drawer.
Maru twists away in embarrassment, and I head back to the center of the drawer.
Chio says she wants to talk about my job as the supervisor. Honestly, I'm scared of hearing what she has to say, but I need to rack up the courage to go. It seems important.
Hey, how are you doing? I was worried you'd be feeling down.
But you seem full of energy. Your voice, your expression... Everywhere but down there, at least.
Ahaha, sorry to tease you. Don't frown like that--it's a good thing.
So, this might be hard, but I need to tell you about Health.
Exactly. So, some people are more energetic than others, right? And some days you're just more tired than normal. That's all part of Health.
You remember what I said about Saeko raising us for her own purposes, right? She wants us all to be healthy.
But little people whose Health drops to 0 or under will pretty much stop moving.
Like a puppet that's had its strings cut. And that's no fun for Saeko, right?
Just keep an eye on everyone's Health. Make sure nobody's Health drops to 0 or worse.
If it looks like somebody might lose all their Health...
You'll need to deal with them, and fast. You get what I'm saying, right?
You're telling me to hand over little people with lower Health to Saeko...right?
Like how Taki made Moko eat the peanut yesterday, since Moko had lower Health than me.
...Exactly. I knew you were a smart one.
Your head's still quick even after all that moving your body did.
I fall silent again in the face of Chio's smirk. I don't know how many times that's been today.
Don't mean to interrupt your fun, but something's up. Maru's acting weird.
I tried talking to him, but he's not responding. Could you take a look, Rin?
Kina calls out while I'm talking to Chio. Apparently, something is off about Maru. I don't know the details, but I should check on him.
Weren't you listening? Maru's acting weird. Why are you talking to me?
I heard you, but I thought I should learn more about the situation first.
I guess that's true. Not that I know all the details or anything.
Maru went into the corner, and now he won't even glance at me when I talk to him. Look...
Um, you didn't do anything, did you, Kina?
Of course I didn't. I was busy trying to think of a way to get out of here when Maru started mumbling to himself over there.
I went to ask him about it, and then he just started shuddering.
Seems like he thinks I'm all scary, so go talk to him instead of me, Rin.
Nobody could be afraid talking to a limp beansprout like you.
Hi, Maru. Are you okay? Did something bad happen?
Maru seems to be having trouble speaking. His feet tremble underneath him.
But you don't look fine... You're shivering even with that cape on for warmth.
...A memory from my childhood, that I've always wished I could forget.
Being shrunk and getting brought here--all of it was destiny.
Maru's words ring through my head.
Could you tell me about what you remembered?
S-Sorry. I didn't mean to shout. But I can't tell anybody about this.
I-I really can't explain, but this must be punishment for what I did in the past.
Yes. And I realized one more thing.
Because of that, I... No, we...
I-I don't know wh-what you're talking about, but l-let's just calm down.
Chio! When did you get here?
Hey, Rin... Isn't it about time to eat?
Saeko will probably be back soon too. You don't have any time to waste.
You need to choose who to feed... And I'm sure you'll make the right decision.
I return to the center of the drawer.
(I know what Chio said. I'm sure that Saeko will kill us if I go against her will.)
(But can I really do the job of the supervisor as Chio's told me to?)
(...Saeko will be back soon. I need to make my decision carefully.)
Chio says it's time to feed somebody. I need someone with at least 50 Appeal while making sure nobody has 0 or worse Health. What should I do?
I approach Chio with the plate of milk. Her eyes grow wide.
I'd like you to drink this milk, Chio. What do you think?
If that's your decision, I'll accept it.

Thank you, Rin. I'm sure it was a hard decision...
You've given it your all.
With resignation, Chio draws her face closer to the rim of the milk plate.
Then, she nods, as if she has accepted her fate...and begins to lick up the milk.
...What is it? I'll blush if you keep looking at me like that.

column1: # 拒否2回目、「誰も美味しそうな人がいない」バッドエンドへ

column2: There's no time left. At this rate, Saeko will kill all of us...
After seeing how Moko was eaten when I gave her the peanut yesterday...
I just can't feed somebody else today...
I know how you feel. The supervisor is really dealt a bad hand.
I wish I could stand in for you...but I'm sure you remember what happened to Taki yesterday.
This is your decision, so we'll have to live with it. Let's enjoy the rest of our time before Saeko comes back.
Rin, would you lie down over there for me? There's this technique I've always wanted to try, so...
Just as Chio seems to be at a loss for words--

column1: # 拒否1回目、もう一度選択画面に戻る

column2: Saeko will want another little person with high Appeal today. You need to feed somebody to satisfy Saeko.
Saeko will be back soon. Make the right decision so that we can all keep living.
...Don't waver. This is your last chance.

column1: # マルにご飯をあげた場合（マルが食べられる、ゲーム続行）

column2: Um, Maru... Are you thirsty? I brought a drink.
There was some milk in the drawer... Do you want it?
Should I really be the one to drink this?
...Yes, I'd like you to drink this, Maru. If you can.
I can hear my heart thumping.
Maru bends down over the rim of the plate and timidly peers inside.
Then, he makes a small nod, as if he has drawn up his resolve...
...and laps up the liquid within with his tongue, like a cat would.
As Maru drinks, his trembling worsens. The eyes he had closed are now wide open.
E-Everyone's going to be killed--all because of me...
It's exactly like it was in my old memory...
What do you keep yapping about? Stop talking about us getting killed. It's bad luck.
...Urk. I guess I need to explain.
We're going to die here covered in pee. We'll be thrown into an ocean of yellow and drown...
But it's going to happen. It's punishment for what I did in the past.
When I was in elementary school, I drank a lot of milk. I think a cold was going around, so there was extra milk left over at lunch...
I used to love milk, so I drank a ton of it. But then, on the way home, I just couldn't hold it in...
And I ran into the bushes and did my business.
I held it in as long as I could.
But it kept coming out. I couldn't stop it. I was worried I'd get my feet wet, so I looked at the ground...
...and it had turned into a little river, flowing down toward an anthill.
The liquid just swirled right into the hole. Like the drain in a bathtub.
And then...I started using it as a target.
I can't make any excuses for what I did... I think it was just entertainment for me.
Finally, the hole filled up, and the dirt collapsed to form a lake...
Before I'd noticed, a puddle had formed, and little bubbles started rising, along with what looked like white ant eggs.
I had stolen the lives of a whole nest of insects. And enjoyed it, like a game.
Ever since then...I haven't been able to drink milk, and I grew to hate violence. Whenever the memory comes back to me, I'm filled with regret...
But I'd forgotten recently. And now, I've been shrunk...
At first, I thought it was just a coincidence--but now, I know.
This is my punishment. Because I'd forgotten my past sin.
Urgh... I'm so sorry. This is all my fault...
Maru starts sniffling. Just as I begin to wonder about how I should comfort him--

column1: # キナにご飯をあげた場合（マル衰弱、バッドエンド）

column2: Huh? What are you bringing me milk for?
Trying to be nice? I don't actually like milk though.
Uh... Yeah, trying to be nice.
I can hear my heart thumping.
You might not like the taste, but could you drink it all for me? To keep up your health.
Fine, I'll give it a go. Health's important, like you say.
Kina bends down over the rim of the plate and brings her head closer to the milk inside.
She laps up the liquid within with her tongue...like a cat would.
Uh...this pose is really embarrassing for me. Could you at least look the other way while I'm drinking?
I turn my face down and away, just as I was told, but I look up when I notice a strange presence...
...and find Maru standing there, his face grim.
M-Maru? What's wrong? Why do you look like that?
Saeko's gonna kill all of us...
Maru's voice is almost too quiet to hear. His whole body shakes violently.
Even after I call out to him, he just shakes his head slightly. His lips keep moving as he mumbles.
I don't know what to do. Just as I approach Maru to try to shake him out of it--
A low, muffled vibration reverberates from a distance. The sound of heavy footsteps.
There's no mistaking it. Saeko has returned to her room.
Saeko walks through the room with even breaths. Every step she takes shakes the drawer like an earthquake...
Each tremor growing larger and more violent than the last.
Soon, they become too strong for me to stand.
I find myself crouching on the floor.
Next, I hear the creak of something moving.
Then, a tremendous commotion that shakes the whole room...
...as the room we're in--the second drawer of a desk--is pried open by Saeko's fingers.
Hello, cute little people. Were you good today while I was away?
Sorry I'm late today. I took a little detour before coming back.
But the wind was strong, so now I'm cold...
Could I have another one of you today? As a treat.
Saeko's hand comes down into the drawer.
Her unthinkably large fingers fill our vision.
They wander for just a moment, as if carefully making a decision...

column1: # キナに食べ物をあげたルート。マル衰弱、ゲームオーバー

column2: And then, they stop. Right in front of Kina's body.
Her gigantic fingers wrap around Kina's tiny arms and legs...
...and carry her up into the sky.
I look where Saeko is pointing and see Maru...
...whose expression is lifeless, as he stares out at nothing.
...*sigh* He's useless to me like this.
Rin. I need my dear little people to be full of energy.
If you can't even do this much...
...then I don't need you in my world.
And then return to the sky, empty-handed.
Nobody's making my mouth water today.
I suppose you were just an ordinary human after all.
...Whatever. I don't need any of you anymore.

column1: # マルにあげたルート、死んでキャラ選択画面に戻る

column2: And then, they stop. Right in front of Maru's body.
I know the truth... You're planning on killing all of us!
You trapped us in this small place s-so that you can p-pee on top of us...
A-And we'll all drown to death, stuck in this drawer!
I'm sorry for what I did before! So please! Don't be so cruel!

column1: # 冴子の表情差分で驚き顔作ってもいいかも

column2: Wait, this isn't what I tho...
Heehee. He was so full of energy. And fun.
And that taste--a hidden flavor within a soft interior...
You did wonderful today. I can tell you were really trying.
Good job. Let's talk again later.
